stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x^{3} + ([[b]])x^{2} + ([[c]])x + ([[d]]) \). What is the value of \( f''([[x0]]) \), which tells you the concavity of \( f \) at \( x=[[x0]] \)?
distractor_rationales: This is \( f'([[x0]]) \), the first derivative (slope), not the second derivative.
This drops the factor of 2 on the constant term \( 2[[b]] \) of \( f'' \).
This evaluates \( f([[x0]]) \), the function value, not its second derivative.
This omits the constant term \( 2[[b]] \) of \( f'' \) entirely, keeping only the \( x \)-dependent part's coefficient.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: A firm's marginal cost model gives total cost \( C(x) = [[a]]x^{3} + ([[p]])x^{2} \) (in thousands of dollars) for producing \( x \) thousand units. For \( x \) beyond what value does the cost curve \( C \) switch from concave down to concave up?
solution: \( C''(x) = 6[[a]]x + 2([[p]]) \), which is zero (and changes sign) at \( x = -\dfrac{[[p]]}{3[[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of 3 in the denominator, using \( C'(x) \)'s structure instead of \( C''(x) \)'s.
This has the wrong sign on the inflection point.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value incorrectly (dividing by 6 instead of 3).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: \( f'(x)=1-\dfrac{[[a]]}{x^{2}} \), and \( f''(x)=\dfrac{2[[a]]}{x^{3}} \). Evaluating at \( x=[[x0]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( f'([[x0]]) \), the first derivative, not the second.
This has the wrong sign and power, mixing up the first-derivative structure.
This forgets the factor of 2 that appears when differentiating \( -a/x^{2} \) again.
This has the correct factor of 2 but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = x^{3} + kx^{2} + ([[b]])x \), where \( k \) is a constant. If \( f \) has an inflection point at \( x = [[x0]] \), what is the value of \( k \)?
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of 2 on \( k \), solving \( 6x_{0}+k=0 \) instead.
This has the wrong sign and divides instead of multiplying by 3.
This doubles the correct coefficient.
This mistakenly halves \( x_{0}\) instead of multiplying by \( -3 \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
